skill.name: brand-guidelines-community
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-inspired brand colors, typography, and visual styling guidance to documents, presentations, and design artifacts.
skill.summary: Guide artifacts toward Anthropic-style colors, fonts, text hierarchy, and accent usage.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but review found false positives in Markdown color code spans and Apache License text. The skill contains only brand styling guidance and license text, with no executable code, runtime network calls, shell commands, or prompt injection content. No confirmed security issue was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Apache License URLs are non-runtime references
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links. They do not instruct the assistant to make network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are inside standard license text. No runtime behavior or network-capable code is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: license text misclassified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged license lines contain legal terms such as contribution, NOTICE, trademarks, warranty, and file description guidance. They are not cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is the unmodified Apache License. No hashing, encryption, or crypto library calls appear in these lines.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown color literals are not shell execution
low_findings.description: The backticks around hex color values are Markdown inline code formatting. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown list items documenting brand colors. There is no Ruby file, script block, or executable command context.

low_findings.title: False positive: brand guidance terms are not weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged SKILL.md lines describe brand styling, keywords, font fallback, and usage scope. They do not reference weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is descriptive Markdown about visual identity. No cryptographic operation or dependency is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: style terminology is not reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a gray color label and an RGBColor documentation reference. These are presentation styling details, not system or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a visual design guide. There are no commands, probes, sockets, host lookups, or system inventory instructions.

content.value_statement: Design artifacts can drift from a consistent brand system. This skill provides clear color, typography, hierarchy, and accent guidance for Anthropic-style presentation work.
actual_capabilities: Provides Anthropic-style primary, neutral, and accent color values.
Defines heading and body typography choices with fallback fonts.
Guides text hierarchy using heading size, body text, and readable contrast.
Suggests accent color cycling for non-text shapes and visual elements.
Explains how to maintain brand-like color fidelity across artifacts.
limitations: Does not include executable automation or a presentation conversion script.
Does not install Poppins, Lora, or any other font files.
Does not verify official trademark permission for external publication.
Does not include full official brand assets such as logos or imagery.

use_cases.title: Presentation polish
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, typography, and accent treatment to a slide deck before stakeholder review.

use_cases.title: Document styling
use_cases.description: Convert a plain report into a visually consistent artifact with defined headings, body text, and neutral backgrounds.

use_cases.title: Design QA
use_cases.description: Review an existing artifact against documented colors, font choices, hierarchy, and accent usage.

prompt_templates.title: Style a simple document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for brand-aligned formatting guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand styling guidance to this document. Focus on colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and readable contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh a presentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for slide-level visual cleanup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slide deck and suggest brand-aligned updates for headings, body text, backgrounds, shapes, and accent colors.

prompt_templates.title: Create a brand checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured review before publishing an artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise brand styling checklist for this artifact. Include color use, typography, hierarchy, accents, and accessibility concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Audit visual consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across multiple pages or slides.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this artifact for visual consistency. Identify where it departs from the brand guidance and propose specific corrections by section.

output_examples.input: A plain project update deck with inconsistent colors and mixed fonts.
output: Use dark text on light backgrounds for primary content.
Apply Poppins or Arial to slide titles and section headings.
Use Lora or Georgia for body text where readability allows.
Reserve orange, blue, and green accents for shapes and key callouts.

output_examples.input: A report page with weak hierarchy and several decorative colors.
output: Limit the palette to the documented neutral and accent colors.
Increase heading contrast and keep body text in a readable neutral color.
Use one accent color per section to reduce visual noise.

output_examples.input: A design review request for an artifact intended to resemble Anthropic styling.
output_examples.output: The review would compare colors, fonts, heading hierarchy, shape accents, and fallback behavior against the documented guidance.

best_practices: Use the documented colors consistently instead of introducing nearby alternatives.
Keep headings and body text in distinct font roles for clear hierarchy.
Check contrast after applying light, dark, and accent colors.
anti_patterns: Do not use the guidance as proof of trademark permission or endorsement.
Do not mix many accent colors in the same small section.
Do not rely on custom fonts without checking fallback rendering.

faq.question: Does this skill contain official brand assets?
faq.answer: No. It documents colors, typography, and styling guidance, but it does not include logos or official asset files.

faq.question: Can it automatically edit PowerPoint files?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance only. It mentions presentation styling concepts but includes no automation script.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files do not contain runtime network requests or instructions to fetch external resources.

faq.question: Are the fonts installed by the skill?
faq.answer: No. It recommends Poppins and Lora with Arial and Georgia fallbacks when those fonts are unavailable.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish from a security perspective?
faq.answer: Yes. The static findings were reviewed as false positives, and no confirmed security issue was found.
